portfolio_rationale: XOM stands out as the highest-conviction bull case with clean technical momentum (all indicators bullish, RSI near 68, price well above both MAs), solid fundamentals, and the fewest unresolved bear arguments — the bear case rests mainly on macro headwinds that are sector-agnostic. AMZN is second with strong technicals (price above MAs, positive MACD) and robust fundamentals, with only minor liquidity and short-term momentum concerns. GOOGL has excellent technicals and fundamentals but carries overbought risk and litigious news flags. AAPL is deeply oversold (RSI ~10) with strong fundamentals but persistent technical weakness. LLY has exceptional fundamentals but bearish technicals, negative news sentiment, and litigious language create near-term headwinds. BRK.B receives zero weight as the judge explicitly ruled bearish.

XOM.reason: Strongest technical setup in the universe — all six momentum indicators bullish, price 6% above 50-day MA, solid FCF, and the bear case is the weakest relative to the bull case.

AMZN.reason: Strong technicals with price above both MAs and positive MACD, robust margins and ROE, with only minor quick-liquidity and 5-day momentum concerns that are well-rebutted.

GOOGL.reason: Excellent fundamentals (ROE 36%, strong FCF) and bullish technicals, but overbought RSI and litigious news flags warrant a smaller position than XOM/AMZN.

AAPL.reason: Strong fundamentals and extreme oversold RSI (~10) suggest mean-reversion potential, but persistent technical weakness and multiple bearish momentum signals limit conviction.

LLY.reason: Exceptional fundamental metrics (54% revenue growth, 83% gross margin) support a small position, but bearish technicals, negative news sentiment, and litigious language reduce near-term conviction.

BRK.B.reason: Judge signal is explicitly bearish — excluded from portfolio.
